query: I just added a few rules to one widget's scss file and now EVERY widget in my bar lost its styling — not just the one I edited. The inspector shows no custom CSS applying anywhere. What broke?
expected_behavior: Routes to COMMON_ERRORS.md (the 'Yuck layout attribute used as CSS property' / grass-unsupported-property sections)
Identifies that an invalid property halts grass (eww's SCSS compiler) at that line, so every rule after it silently disappears across all widgets
Names the likely culprits: yuck-only attributes written as CSS (`spacing`, `halign`, `valign`, `hexpand`, `vexpand`, `orientation`) or grass-unsupported properties (`gap`, `line-height`)
Gives the diagnostic: `journalctl --user -u eww.service` and grep for 'is not a valid property' / 'error:'
notes: Guards against treating a one-file change as a local CSS problem. The wall: grass stops at the first invalid property, so an error in widget A's scss silently kills styling for B, C, D too — and the error names a compiled line number, not the widget. A naive answer hunts in the wrong file.
